title: Church of Nerv in India WTFOMGBBQ
display_title: Church of Nerv in India WTFOMGBBQ
wikitext:
[]: "Church of Nerv" in India (WTFOMGBBQ)             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Jun 30, 2009 10:41 am This is a good place to start: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YGB0XaDYXCI The Youtube channel with more: http://www.youtube.com/user/CHURCHOFNERV I don't know if this has been posted before, or even if it is real, but this seems to be a religious movement that has aped some of the visuals/terms from NGE to... well... I don't know. O_O Fake or not, it made me giggle.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:37 am (Racist post deleted)

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:42 am That it beyond creepy. Maybe they saw the show and interpereted it somehow. Either that or it is some kind of wierd publicity stunt.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:51 am i'm scared

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:54 am Anno trolled India with fake religion.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:56 am Almost reminds me of Matrixism and The Church of Ed Wood . It amazes me that many "religions" today aren't even trying to base themselves off of some sort of historical text or account anymore. We'll just turn on the TV and worship whatever's on. I'm waiting for Oprah to have a religion. It isn't her hard core "followers" don't already consider her magazines sacred anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Tue Jun 30, 2009 12:00 pm You can say whatever you want, is still better than scientology.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 30, 2009 12:02 pm Rock-Slash wrote: You can say whatever you want, is still better than scientology.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Jun 30, 2009 12:07 pm In India, you can find a religion of pretty much anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Jun 30, 2009 12:32 pm This reminds me of a plot point in the as of now unifinished part 3 of "Tales from that place over there" I'm currently writing. Lets just hope that church isn't run by people eating lost children from another dimension.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Tue Jun 30, 2009 1:07 pm Wes Anderson wrote: This (Evangelion) could spawn something like Scientology.

[]:             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Tue Jun 30, 2009 1:26 pm i am officially scared so when india launches an attack on the U.S with giant robots called evangelion claiming we have adam or the lance we know where they got it from huh lol i am totally jk on this

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Jun 30, 2009 1:41 pm Yay, one more apocalyptic panic mongerer. Not much different at heart than others. Just groovier graphics.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:15 pm They have the date of Second Impact (which they seem to equate with the Second Coming) "scheduled" for 25/12/2012...

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:24 pm So there is a church of NERV huh, kinda funny. 2012..Argh, don't get started on that.

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:28 pm what the hell!? i mean, when this is real and i look at the first vid i wonder, did they even tried? don't realy know how to react on this... (schould i join? or schould i scream heretic? or simply laugh?) atleast know we know why india wanted this nuclear programm and what their planning to do with it...

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:29 pm NERV has a church, now! And it is in India?! I expected this from Betterman but from Eva? Although given the fanbase, I shouldn't be too surprised at this.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:40 pm It won't be long for places like this to start popping up in tons different countries. That wouldn't be suprising if this kind of place has already popped up in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:55 pm I'm thinking it's just a christian ministry that got a little creative with the cross-marketing potential of rebuild. Yes, pun definately intended.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Jun 30, 2009 9:28 pm LiLi wrote: They have the date of Second Impact (which they seem to equate with the Second Coming) "scheduled" for 25/12/2012...
